title: Diasperus samarensis
taxonomy: Diasperus samarensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is native to the Philippines and is also known as the Samarensis tree or Samarensis Diospyros. This plant is classified as a type of evergreen tree and is characterized by its smooth gray bark and simple, opposite leaves. The leaves are elliptical in shape and have a glossy appearance. The plant is often used as an ornamental tree in gardens and parks due to its attractive foliage and compact growth habit.
uses: Diasperus samarensis is reportedly used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines. The plant is said to possess antimicrobial properties, which can be beneficial in treating various infections. Its leaves may be used to make tea that helps in reducing fever and alleviating pain.
